title: Waste disposal and environmental services managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the work activities and resources necessary for manufacturing products in accordance with cost, quality, and quantity specifications. Plan, direct, or coordinate quality assurance programs. Formulate quality control policies and control quality of laboratory and production efforts. Manage operations at geothermal power generation facilities. Maintain and monitor geothermal plant equipment for efficient and safe plant operations. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Check road or weather conditions to determine how routes will be affected.
Refuel trucks or add other fluids, such as oil or brake fluid.
Organize schedules for refuse collection.
Clean trucks or compactor bodies after routes have been completed.
Tag garbage or recycling containers to inform customers of problems, such as excess garbage or inclusion of items that are not permitted.
Communicate with dispatchers concerning delays, unsafe sites, accidents, equipment breakdowns, or other maintenance problems.
Dismount garbage trucks to collect garbage and remount trucks to ride to the next collection point.

Drive trucks, following established routes, through residential streets or alleys or through business or industrial areas.
Fill out defective equipment reports.
Inspect trucks prior to beginning routes to ensure safe operating condition.
Dump refuse or recyclable materials at disposal sites.
Make special pickups of recyclable materials, such as food scraps, used oil, discarded computers, or other electronic items.
Review logs, datasheets, or reports to ensure adequate production levels and safe production environments or to identify abnormalities with power production equipment or processes.
